data.description:
## Problem
finalize_verdict (orchestrator.py:528) checks coverage['prerequisite_indeterminate'] BEFORE 'elif blocking:'. A subsidiary prerequisite-pass failure therefore downgrades a genuine BLOCK to INDETERMINATE — the author sees a transient 're-run' verdict instead of an actionable 'fix your plan' BLOCK. Both prevent the claim, but a real blocking finding must not be masked by an unrelated prerequisite indeterminacy.

## Fix (maintainer-approved)
Reorder so a genuine blocking finding wins:
    if blocking: verdict = 'BLOCK'
    elif coverage.get('prerequisite_indeterminate'): verdict = 'INDETERMINATE'
    elif coverage.get('llm_unavailable'): ...
Rest of the precedence chain unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- With coverage['prerequisite_indeterminate'] True AND a blocking finding present, verdict == 'BLOCK'.
- With prerequisite_indeterminate True and NO blocking finding, verdict == 'INDETERMINATE' (unchanged).
- Existing llm_unavailable / hierarchy_incomplete / verify_failed precedence unchanged.

## Verification
pytest finalize_verdict: blocking + prerequisite_indeterminate -> BLOCK; prerequisite_indeterminate only -> INDETERMINATE.
data.title: plan-review verdict: a prerequisite-pass indeterminate masks a genuine BLOCK
